Full job description
Salary: £40,000 to £50,000 + quarterly performance bonus (£1,000 per quarter on target, £2,000 on stretch target)

Full-time, UK-based (remote/hybrid) with regular travel

YorLinc is a specialist provider of Non-Medical Help (NMH) services, known in Scotland as Non-Medical Personal Help (NMPH), on a mission to transform the quality and reach of support for disabled students across the UK. We help students access the high-quality academic and personal support they're entitled to through the Disabled Students' Allowance. We're growing quickly, we're ambitious about where we're heading, and we're now looking for a Partnerships Manager to play a central role in that journey, building the relationships that connect more students to the support they need.This is a high-impact role with a high degree of independence, working closely with the Managing Director and with the opportunity to develop into a more strategic leadership position over time. The Role

As Partnerships Manager, you will be the driving force behind YorLinc’s relationships with DSA assessors and university disability teams across the UK. Your focus will be on building trusted partnerships that translate into consistent referral growth, ensuring more disabled students can access the high-quality, personalised support that YorLinc is known for.

You will also take the lead on promoting YorLinc’s UK Research and Innovation (UKRI) DSA delivery service to higher education providers, helping universities manage the administrative and compliance demands of postgraduate student support more effectively.

Key Responsibilities

Build and maintain strong, ongoing relationships with DSA assessors and HEP disability teams
Proactively increase awareness of YorLinc’s NMH services across assessor networks
Drive measurable growth in student referrals through consistent engagement
Develop and deliver CPD sessions and training to assessors and HEP staff, strengthening understanding of YorLinc’s services and supporting referral activity
Promote YorLinc’s UKRI DSA delivery service to universities, positioning it as a solution to reduce administrative burden and improve delivery
Secure and maintain inclusion on university preferred supplier lists
Proactively identify and remove barriers to referral growth
Lead meetings, presentations, and partnership discussions with external stakeholders
Track engagement and referral activity, reporting on outcomes and opportunities
Collaborate with internal operations and delivery teams to ensure capacity and quality keep pace with referral growth
What Success Looks Like

A clear and measurable increase in awareness of YorLinc across assessor networks
Consistent growth in weekly student referrals
Strong, trusted partnerships with HEPs and assessors that generate ongoing activity
Universities actively engaged in YorLinc’s UKRI DSA delivery service
A direct and visible contribution to NMH growth and revenue
What We’re Looking For

Experience in DSA, NMH, higher education, or a closely related environment (strongly preferred)
Candidates from partnerships, account management, business development, or stakeholder engagement backgrounds are equally encouraged to apply
Strong relationship-building skills and the ability to influence external stakeholders
Confidence delivering presentations, training sessions, or CPD
A self-starter who thrives with autonomy and takes genuine ownership of their work
Able to translate activity into measurable outcomes
Comfortable with regular travel across the UK as part of the role
Salary and Benefits

£40,000 to £50,000 depending on experience
Quarterly performance bonus (up to £8,000 per year)
20 days holiday plus bank holidays plus additional leave over Christmas and New Year
Flexible, remote-based working with the freedom to manage your own schedule and hours, with all equipment provided.
Budget to attend industry events, conferences, and sector networking
Pension
Private medical insurance
Mileage and travel expenses covered
Regular company social events
Why Join YorLinc

YorLinc is at an exciting point in its growth, and this role sits right at the heart of it. You’ll have genuine autonomy, a direct line to the Managing Director, and the freedom to build something meaningful rather than simply executing someone else’s plan.

You’ll also be doing work that matters. Every referral pathway you open, every partnership you build, connects another disabled student to the high-quality, personalised support that YorLinc is known for. That combination of commercial impact and real human purpose is what makes YorLinc a genuinely compelling place to be.

This is also a role with a clear trajectory. As YorLinc grows, so does the opportunity, and we are committed to developing this into a senior leadership position for someone who delivers.

To apply

Please send your CV and a covering letter to partnerships@yorlinc.com. In your covering letter, tell us why this role appeals to you and what you would bring to YorLinc from day one.

Job Types: Full-time, Permanent

personalisedContent.roleNeedsSummary: My read is that this role needs more than relationship management. It needs someone who can open doors, build credibility with specialist stakeholders, and then turn that activity into referrals, training impact and visible growth. It also needs someone who can work independently, keep good records and stay close to operations so promises made externally can be delivered internally.

focusAreasToBring.title: Relationship building with purpose
focusAreasToBring.summary: I would focus on becoming a credible, useful contact for assessors and university teams, not just a name on an email. My aim would be to make it easy for people to understand YorLinc, trust the service and refer with confidence.

focusAreasToBring.title: Clear activity tracking
focusAreasToBring.summary: I would keep close track of meetings, follow-ups, referral patterns and barriers so the work stays measurable. I’m used to using reporting to keep momentum honest.

focusAreasToBring.title: Growth with control
focusAreasToBring.summary: I would stay close to operations and delivery so referral growth is matched by capacity and quality. I’ve worked in environments where growth only worked when the back end was ready.

personalisedContent.fitSummary: I bring a background in improving performance, working with external stakeholders and keeping services controlled in complex environments. While my sector background is broader than higher education, the underlying demands of this role — trust, influence, reporting, training and disciplined follow-through — are familiar to me. I’d expect to add value quickly because I’m comfortable working independently and turning activity into something measurable.
personalisedContent.likelyContributionSummary: Based on what the brief describes, I’d expect to spend the first months building a clear picture of the assessor and university landscape, then creating a steady rhythm of outreach, follow-up and reporting. I’d also want to understand what helps or blocks referrals, so I can remove friction rather than just increase contact. As the role develops, I’d focus on making the partnership activity more consistent, more visible and better connected to delivery capacity.
companyHighlights: YorLinc is a specialist provider of NMH/NMPH services for disabled students.
The role has a direct line to the Managing Director.
The brief signals autonomy, flexible working and a route into senior leadership.
The company is growing quickly and links commercial growth with social purpose.
personalisedContent.cultureFitSummary: I work well in roles where I have ownership, clear expectations and room to build something properly. The mix of autonomy, purpose and accountability in YorLinc feels like a good fit for how I like to work.

first90DaysPlan.detail: I would get close to the current referral routes, key stakeholders, internal processes and any barriers affecting engagement or conversion. I’d want a clear view of where the quickest gains are likely to come from.
I would start structured outreach with assessors and university disability teams, begin meetings and presentations, and put a simple reporting rhythm in place so activity and outcomes are visible.
I would look for patterns in what is generating referrals, tighten follow-up, and work with operations and delivery colleagues to make sure growth is sustainable and service quality stays strong.

personalisedContent.closingSummary: I’d bring YorLinc a practical, accountable approach to partnership growth, with the discipline to turn relationships into results.
closingProofPoints: I improved a live NHS service’s external reputation and patient experience.
I’ve led operations through growth while keeping delivery and reporting under control.
I’ve worked in regulated environments where accuracy, follow-through and stakeholder confidence mattered.
